revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Sn crud]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23991619004|time triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Isaniwa Shrine]][[fr:Isaniwa-jinja]][[ja:伊佐爾波神社]]
{{Infobox religious building | name = {{nihongo|'''Isaniwa Shrine'''|伊佐爾波神社|Isaniwa jinja}} | image = File:Isaniwajinjaroumon.JPG | caption = Approach to Isaniwa Jinja | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{Coord|33.85075|N|132.78891666666667|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|qid=Q812767|1=Shinto}} | type = | deity ={{ill|Chūai|qid=Q179971|1=Emperor Chūai}}; {{ill|Empress Jingū|qid=Q232803|1=Empress Jingū}}; {{ill|Three Goddesses of Munakata|qid=Q10948069|1=Three Munakata Goddesses}}; {{ill|Ōjin|qid=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}} | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = | location = {{ill|Matsuyama|en|Matsuyama|qid=Q200022|lt=Matsuyama|1=Matsuyama}}, {{ill|Ehime Prefecture|en|Ehime Prefecture|qid=Q123376|lt=Ehime Prefecture|1=Ehime Prefecture}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}} | website = | architecture_style ={{ill|Hachiman-zukuri|qid=Q3125455|1=Hachiman-zukuri}} | festival = | leadership = | location = {{ill|Matsuyama|qid=Q200022|1=Matsuyama}} | part_of = {{ill|Q11529766|qid=Q11529766|1=Matsuyama Eight Shrines of Hachiman}} }} [[File:Isaniwa Honden.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Isaniwa [[Honden]] (1667), a rare example of {{ill|Hachiman-zukuri|en|Hachiman-zukuri|qid=Q3125455|lt=Hachiman-zukuri|1=Hachiman-zukuri}} and an {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|qid=Q1188622|lt=Important Cultural Property|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}}]]
{{nihongo|'''Isaniwa Shrine'''|伊佐爾波神社|Isaniwa jinja}} is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|Matsuyama|en|Matsuyama|qid=Q200022|lt=Matsuyama|1=Matsuyama}}, {{ill|Ehime Prefecture|en|Ehime Prefecture|qid=Q123376|lt=Ehime Prefecture|1=Ehime Prefecture}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. Enshrined are {{ill|Emperor Chūai|en|Emperor Chūai|qid=Q179971|lt=Emperor Chūai|1=Emperor Chūai}}, {{ill|Empress Jingū|en|Empress Jingū|qid=Q232803|lt=Empress Jingū|1=Empress Jingū}}, and {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|qid=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ōjin|1=Emperor Ōjin}}. A number of its buildings and treasures have been designated {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|qid=Q1188622|lt=Important Cultural Properties|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}}.
==History== It is said that the shrine was founded on the site where Emperor Chūai and Empress Jingū bathed at {{ill|Dōgo Onsen|en|Dōgo Onsen|qid=Q1037748|lt=Dōgo Onsen|1=Dōgo Onsen}} and it is mentioned in [[:en:Engi shiki|Engi shiki]]. In the fourteenth century the Kōno clan moved the shrine to its present location and it was rebuilt by the {{ill|Matsudaira clan|en|Matsudaira clan|qid=Q1141077|lt=Matsudaira clan|1=Matsudaira clan}} in the seventeenth century. Isaniwa Jinja was restored in 1970.<ref>{{cite web |url=http://isaniwa.11o.jp/top.htm |title=History |publisher=Isaniwa Jinja |accessdate=20 April 2011 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20110722101919/http://isaniwa.11o.jp/top.htm |archive-date=22 July 2011 |url-status=dead }}</ref>
==Buildings== Isaniwa Jinja is modelled upon [[Iwashimizu Hachimangū|Iwashimizu Hachiman-gū]] in {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|en|Kyoto Prefecture|qid=Q120730|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|1=Kyoto Prefecture}} and constructed in the {{ill|Hachiman-zukuri|en|Hachiman-zukuri|qid=Q3125455|lt=Hachiman-zukuri|1=Hachiman-zukuri}} style.<ref>{{cite web |url=http://isaniwa.11o.jp/top.htm |title=Buildings |publisher=Isaniwa Jinja |accessdate=20 April 2011 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20110722101919/http://isaniwa.11o.jp/top.htm |archive-date=22 July 2011 |url-status=dead }}</ref><ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.jnto.go.jp/eng/arrange/attractions/g_route/golden_shikoku.html |title=Isaniwa Jinja |publisher=[[Japan National Tourism Organization]] |accessdate=20 April 2011 |url-status=dead |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20100118194833/http://www.jnto.go.jp/eng/arrange/attractions/g_route/golden_shikoku.html |archivedate=18 January 2010 }}</ref> * [[Honden]] (1667) ({{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|qid=Q1188622|lt=Important Cultural Property|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}})<ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.city.matsuyama.ehime.jp/kybunka/1175922_1047.html |title=Isaniwa Jinja Honden |publisher={{ill|Matsuyama|en|Matsuyama|qid=Q200022|lt=Matsuyama City|1=Matsuyama}} |accessdate=20 April 2011 |url-status=dead |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20110927081359/http://www.city.matsuyama.ehime.jp/kybunka/1175922_1047.html |archivedate=27 September 2011 }}</ref><ref>{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/102/3344 |title=Database of Registered National Cultural Properties |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=20 April 2011}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |url=http://ehime-c.esnet.ed.jp/bunkazai/kennobunkazai/shiteibunkazai/kenzoubutsu/pdf-files/kuni/1-1-19isaniwajinja.pdf |title=Isaniwa Jinja |publisher={{ill|Ehime Prefecture|en|Ehime Prefecture|qid=Q123376|lt=Ehime Prefecture|1=Ehime Prefecture}} |accessdate=20 April 2011}}</ref> * Mōshidono corridor (1667) (ICP)<ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.city.matsuyama.ehime.jp/kybunka/1175923_1047.html |title=Isaniwa Jinja Mōshidono Corridor |publisher={{ill|Matsuyama|en|Matsuyama|qid=Q200022|lt=Matsuyama City|1=Matsuyama}} |accessdate=20 April 2011 |url-status=dead |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20110927081406/http://www.city.matsuyama.ehime.jp/kybunka/1175923_1047.html |archivedate=27 September 2011 }}</ref><ref>{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/102/3345 |title=Database of Registered National Cultural Properties |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=20 April 2011}}</ref> * {{ill|Rōmon|en|Rōmon|qid=Q3458957|lt=Rōmon|1=Rōmon}} (1667) (ICP)<ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.city.matsuyama.ehime.jp/kybunka/1175924_1047.html |title=Isaniwa Jinja Rōmon |publisher={{ill|Matsuyama|en|Matsuyama|qid=Q200022|lt=Matsuyama City|1=Matsuyama}} |accessdate=20 April 2011 |url-status=dead |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20110927081422/http://www.city.matsuyama.ehime.jp/kybunka/1175924_1047.html |archivedate=27 September 2011 }}</ref><ref>{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/102/3346 |title=Database of Registered National Cultural Properties |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=20 April 2011}}</ref> * {{ill|Kairō|en|Kairō|qid=Q7884494|lt=Kairō|1=Kairō}} (1667) (ICP)<ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.city.matsuyama.ehime.jp/kybunka/1175925_1047.html |title=Isaniwa Jinja Kairō |publisher={{ill|Matsuyama|en|Matsuyama|qid=Q200022|lt=Matsuyama City|1=Matsuyama}} |accessdate=20 April 2011 |url-status=dead |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20110927081430/http://www.city.matsuyama.ehime.jp/kybunka/1175925_1047.html |archivedate=27 September 2011 }}</ref><ref>{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/102/3347 |title=Database of Registered National Cultural Properties |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=20 April 2011}}</ref> * [[Setsumatsusha|Massha]] (ICP)<ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.city.matsuyama.ehime.jp/kybunka/1175926_1047.html |title=Isaniwa Jinja Massha |publisher={{ill|Matsuyama|en|Matsuyama|qid=Q200022|lt=Matsuyama City|1=Matsuyama}} |accessdate=20 April 2011 |url-status=dead |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20110927081439/http://www.city.matsuyama.ehime.jp/kybunka/1175926_1047.html |archivedate=27 September 2011 }}</ref>
==Treasures== A treasure hall houses a number of swords and suits of armour.<ref>{{cite web |url=http://isaniwa.11o.jp/top.htm |title=Treasure Hall |publisher=Isaniwa Jinja |accessdate=20 April 2011 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20110722101919/http://isaniwa.11o.jp/top.htm |archive-date=22 July 2011 |url-status=dead }}</ref> * [[Tachi]] ({{ill|Kamakura period|en|Kamakura period|qid=Q236205|lt=Kamakura period|1=Kamakura period}}) (ICP)<ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.city.matsuyama.ehime.jp/kybunka/1175995_1047.html |title=Isaniwa Jinja tachi |publisher={{ill|Matsuyama|en|Matsuyama|qid=Q200022|lt=Matsuyama City|1=Matsuyama}} |accessdate=20 April 2011 |url-status=dead |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20110927081453/http://www.city.matsuyama.ehime.jp/kybunka/1175995_1047.html |archivedate=27 September 2011 }}</ref><ref>{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/6378 |title=Database of Registered National Cultural Properties |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=20 April 2011}}</ref>
==See also== {{Commons Category|Isaniwa-jinja}} * [[Iwashimizu Hachimangū|Iwashimizu Hachiman-gū]]
